risks: Standard_LRS storage provides no geo-redundancy for the central data lake — data loss risk if the region has a storage failure
Public network access enabled on ADF and Synapse workspace with no managed VNet or private endpoints — exposes services to internet
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings) — limits operational visibility
Databricks Standard tier lacks Unity Catalog for data governance and fine-grained access control
DW100c is the minimum dedicated SQL pool tier — may be insufficient for production query workloads

No explicit RBAC role assignment resources for managed identity permissions (Storage Blob Data Contributor) — deployment will succeed but services cannot access storage without post-deployment configuration
correctionsRecommended: Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on all resources for operational monitoring
Enable managed virtual network on Synapse workspace (set managedVirtualNetwork to 'default') and add private endpoints for network isolation
Add explicit RBAC role assignment resources granting Storage Blob Data Contributor to ADF, Databricks, and Synapse managed identities on the ADLS Gen2 account
Upgrade storage redundancy from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for production durability
Add resource tags (e.g., environment, cost-center, project) for cost tracking and operational management
Consider upgrading Databricks to Premium tier if Unity Catalog governance is needed for multi-team data access
